fixed_basis: pre-calibrated once at phi=pi; no per-step optimization

pre_registered_predictions.P1_gamma1_null: PASS: gamma=1 has no membrane signal and no pass/block change in all scenarios.
pre_registered_predictions.P2_stress_membrane: PASS: stress gamma=0 and gamma=0.25 show Bell-excess membrane signal, A passage increase, B leak suppression, D stress suppression, and release change.
pre_registered_predictions.P3_contaminated_stress: PASS: contaminated stress keeps the same selectivity and shows B leak suppression under a higher B load.
pre_registered_predictions.P4_normal_storage_specificity: PASS: normal/storage have no membrane Bell-excess signal and no pass/block change.
pre_registered_predictions.P5_replay_specificity: PASS: matched replay reproduces downstream release, so post-gate dynamics follow the gate trace.

safe_interpretation: A fixed-basis finite-shot CHSH measurement-boundary signal can directly modulate membrane pass/block decisions in stress contexts: A passage increases while B contaminant and D stress passage are suppressed. Matched replay shows downstream dynamics follow the gate trace; specificity remains at the Bell-bound measurement/decision boundary.
limitations: Not chemical realism or biological metabolism.
The membrane decision rule is a model component, not a discovered biological membrane mechanism.
Post-gate dynamics are classical-effective once the membrane gate trace is fixed.
Ordinary local population plumbing remains classical-effective.
